economic_notes: Post year-end commitment of up to $11.95M for wind energy project (Dune-du-Nord at Iles-de-la-Madeleine). Also committed up to $3.5M for beluga whale observation site (90% government funded). Commercial revenues from fishing (39% of revenue), maple syrup operations (Erabliere Malecite S.E.C.). Partnerships in Salaweg inc. (seaweed processing, 33%) and Erabliere du Lac de l'Ile inc. (maple syrup, 50%). Economic dependency on government grants (46%) and fishing revenues (39%).
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2022. Financial statements are in French (Quebec). Three fully consolidated subsidiaries: Société de développement Wulustuk inc., Érablière Malécite S.E.C., and Société de gestion Toku. Two equity-method partnerships: Salaweg inc. (33%) and Érablière du Lac de l'Île inc. (50%). Accounting policy change applied retrospectively regarding government assistance previously netted against capital assets. Restricted cash includes $6,166,963 in trust account related to fisheries access rights. Federal trust funds of $335,159 held in Canadian government treasury. Large post-year-end commitments for wind energy ($11.95M) and beluga observation site ($3.5M). No remuneration schedule included in the financial statements provided. Revenue total reconciliation: government transfers $4,704,579 + commercial activities $4,249,741 + interest income $5,213 + other revenues $1,210,546 - losses on disposals $32,857 = $10,137,222.
